epics.title: Separate songs index and song detail routes

tasks.title: Make /songs a blog-like song index
acceptance_criteria: /songs presents the collection as a navigable index without embedding a selected song detail pane.
Each song item links to a stable per-song URL.
The index keeps enough metadata for scanning without duplicating detail-page content.
done_definition: Rendered /songs browser evidence shows a list/index surface only.
The list has meaningful empty-state behavior if song data is absent.

tasks.title: Add a per-song detail route
acceptance_criteria: A dynamic song detail route renders the selected song by id.
Unknown song ids resolve through the App Router not-found path.
Detail pages include navigation back to the song index.
done_definition: A representative route such as /songs/janhyang-photo-sample loads directly in the browser.
Next.js build succeeds with the dynamic route.

epics.title: Remove redundant labels from song detail

tasks.title: Simplify detail metadata and notes
acceptance_criteria: Detail metadata is presented as concise values instead of label-heavy pairs where the meaning is obvious.
The detail header focuses on title, artist, summary, and essential song metadata.
Chart notes remain available only when they add useful song-specific context.
done_definition: Browser evidence for a representative detail page shows no redundant local labels such as Source, Sections, Systems, or Chart notes headings when the content is self-evident.
The lead-sheet renderer still exposes section structure and musical navigation marks.

tasks.title: Verify lead sheet visual fit
acceptance_criteria: Representative lead-sheet rows fit the available pane width at desktop viewport.
The page avoids unnecessary hard minimum widths or avoidable horizontal overflow around labels.
No new reusable semantic CSS classes are introduced without served CSS verification.
done_definition: Post-change browser screenshots are captured for /songs and a representative detail route.
Lint, build, and AI-team validation pass or failures are recorded as blockers.

assumptions: The existing song data remains the source of truth; this task changes routing and presentation rather than transcription content.
The user's '잡초' refers to redundant explanatory labels and local headings on the detail surface, not musical markings such as section names, repeats, endings, D.S., or Fine.
The uncommitted lead-sheet renderer already present in the shared checkout is relevant context for the requested song detail cleanup.
risks: The current shared checkout contains related uncommitted songs changes; these are preserved in the shared checkout and copied into the task worktree for continuity.
If the detail route changes client navigation assumptions, existing client-only workspace code may need to become a simpler server-rendered index.
Visual changes can pass build checks while still feeling cluttered, so browser evidence and design review are required.
